meta.title: Fairyland Kaneyama Ski Area
meta.description: Tadami Line Oku-Aizu—photo-forward LP linking rail tourism and winter scenery.

hero.title:
Fairyland Kaneyama
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Local powder without the queues—then Kitakata ramen and Netsu-shio Onsen.

night.label: Hours
night.note: Night skiing on Course 2 when open

yotei.label: View
yotei.note: Sea of clouds on clear days

transit.title:
Ski · eat · soak.
The Kitakata loop.
transit.lead: About 30 minutes by car from JR Kitakata Station. Morning laps on quiet powder, afternoon ramen and hot springs—a base for day trips around Aizu and Nekoma without mega-resort crowds.
transit.imgAlt: Fairyland Kaneyama Ski Area overlooking the Aizu Basin—a hub for regional loops

primary.title: Local powder · no crowds
primary.lead: North-facing slopes hold snow well, and fresh tracks can linger after storms on this compact hill. Ideal if you want your own pace away from lift lines and tracked-out mega-resort runs.
primary.imgAlt: Untouched powder and a panorama over the Aizu Basin

secondary.title: Kitakata ramen & Netsu-shio Onsen
secondary.lead: After skiing, head to Kitakata's famous ramen shops or Netsu-shio Onsen, about 10 minutes by car—plan a half-day or overnight trip with food and baths, not just lifts.

pass.title:
Adults ¥2,500,
4-hour ¥1,500.
pass.lead: 8-hour tickets (adult ¥2,500 · child ¥1,800) and 4-hour tickets (adult ¥1,500)—affordable compared with major Aizu resorts. Third Sunday of each month: Kids' Ski Day (child ¥1,000).
pass.noticeBody: Base elevation is around 570 m—warm winters can affect snow and opening days. Check today's operations before you leave.
pass.imgAlt: Center house and approach from the Kitakata side

rental.title:
Yamagoya Kurara
for meals and breaks.
rental.lead: Warm food and rest at restaurant Yamagoya Kurara. Enjoy views over the Aizu Basin from the hilltop lodge.
rental.whisper: Mobile signal can be weak in the mountains.
rental.imgAlt: Yamagoya Kurara lodge with a wood-stove atmosphere

yukinko.title:
After storms,
fresh snow can last past noon.
yukinko.lead: With fewer visitors than major resorts, untracked snow sometimes lingers on powder days—especially in ungroomed areas popular with local riders.

family.title:
Mostly gentle slopes,
three easy-to-navigate courses.
family.lead: Three beginner- to intermediate-focused courses. Two lifts keep the hill compact—good for families and first-timers. Forest Course (~1,500 m) suits relaxed cruising.
family.noticeBody: Afternoons pair well with Kitakata ramen and Netsu-shio Onsen—an easy family day plan from one base.
family.imgAlt: Families on gentle slopes at Fairyland Kaneyama Ski Area

workation.title:
Morning ski,
afternoon ramen and onsen.
workation.lead: Rent a car from Kitakata Station—ski in the morning, finish with Kitakata ramen and Netsu-shio Onsen. A classic regional day loop.

03.title: Aizu Basin view
03.body: Sea of clouds on clear days

05.title: Netsu-shio Onsen
05.body: ~10 min by car · hidden hot spring

local.title: Aizu local powder primer
local.body: Skip Nekoma crowds and ski for ¥2,500—how Sanokura fits the Aizu map and what to expect for snow.

schools.title: Kitakata ramen & kura streets
schools.body: Birthplace of Kitakata ramen—where to eat after skiing and a short walk through the historic warehouse district.

summer.title: Netsu-shio Onsen & flower fields
summer.body: Hot springs in winter; Sanokura plateau flower fields in summer and autumn—the same hill transforms off-season.

access.line: 857-1 Kitagongenmori A, Aida, Atsushio Kano-machi, Kitakata, Fukushima
access.note: ~30 min by car from JR Kitakata Station · ~400 free parking spaces
access.rentacarLink: Book a rental car at Shinjo Station
access.rentacarHint: For Kaneyama and Tadami Line loops

footer.location: 857-1 Kitagongenmori A, Aida, Atsushio Kano-machi, Kitakata, Fukushima 966-0108
